client_description: AI-assisted page builder for non-technical creators. $27-47/mo. Tagline: 'Digital-Business-in-a-Box, zero tech skills required.'
category: Funnel builders / Landing page builders / Course platforms

clickfunnels.name: ClickFunnels
clickfunnels.tagline: You're one funnel away from changing the world
clickfunnels.positioning: Premium funnel-builder ecosystem with community and education. 100K+ users, 1M+ funnels created.
clickfunnels.audience: Solopreneurs, coaches, course creators, online marketers
clickfunnels.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: Premium price point, established brand, complete ecosystem vs PageWheel's $27 starter and AI-first templates

leadpages.name: Leadpages
leadpages.tagline: Build pages that convert. No developer needed.
leadpages.positioning: AI landing pages, smart traffic routing, A/B testing. Marketers + agencies.
leadpages.audience: Marketers, agencies, SaaS, ecommerce, performance marketing teams
leadpages.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: Page-builder DNA without the full funnel/checkout/community layer; higher price, more marketing-focused

kartra.name: Kartra
kartra.tagline: Earn more with the most powerful all-in-one platform
kartra.positioning: All-in-one platform: pages + email + courses + funnels + affiliates + webinars
kartra.audience: Coaches, experts, creators, consultants, course creators
kartra.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: Heavier all-in-one stack at higher price; PageWheel wins on simplicity and price

kajabi.name: Kajabi
kajabi.tagline: Turn What You Know Into What You're Known For
kajabi.positioning: Premium knowledge-business platform: courses, coaching, communities, memberships. $11B+ earned by users.
kajabi.audience: Experts, coaches, solopreneurs building knowledge businesses
kajabi.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: Premium tier ($149+); PageWheel competes on price and template-first quick-launch

systemeio.name: Systeme.io
systemeio.tagline: The only tool you need to launch your online business
systemeio.positioning: Free-tier-led all-in-one platform. Direct price competitor to PageWheel.
systemeio.audience: Entrepreneurs launching online businesses; freelancers, educators, creators
systemeio.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: MOST DIRECT COMPETITOR on pricing ($27/mo entry matches PageWheel exactly). Differentiator: PageWheel emphasizes AI templates + done-for-you setup; Systeme.io emphasizes free tier and feature breadth.

gohighlevel.name: GoHighLevel
gohighlevel.positioning: Agency-focused white-label platform; CRM + funnels + email + SMS + calendars
gohighlevel.audience: Marketing agencies and SaaS resellers (not direct creators)
gohighlevel.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: Agency tool, not creator tool. Different buyer entirely — included as adjacent competitor for branded keyword overlap.

base44.name: base44
base44.tagline: AI no-code platform — build apps with natural language
base44.positioning: AI-powered no-code app builder. Closest spiritual sibling to PageWheel's AI-first approach, but app-focused vs page-focused.
base44.audience: Non-technical entrepreneurs building internal tools, portals, productivity apps
base44.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: App-focused (workflows, internal tools) vs PageWheel's page/funnel focus. Adjacent — both AI-first for non-coders.

elementor.name: Elementor
elementor.tagline: The industry-leading visual website builder for WordPress
elementor.positioning: 4M+ active WordPress installs. Drag-and-drop with integrated AI tools. Different category entirely (WordPress-dependent) but bids broadly on page-builder keywords.
elementor.audience: WordPress users — web creators, agencies, freelancers, enterprises
elementor.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: WordPress-dependent (requires WP install + hosting). PageWheel is standalone all-in-one. Different buyer entirely; PageWheel wins simplicity, Elementor wins for WP-committed users.

lovable.name: Lovable
lovable.tagline: Build something Lovable — AI app builder via conversation
lovable.positioning: Conversational AI app builder. Hot AI startup positioning. Targets broad builder personas from students to enterprise teams.
lovable.audience: Students, founders, product managers, designers, marketers, enterprise teams
lovable.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: App-builder positioning (more general-purpose) vs PageWheel's specific creator/coach niche. Big AI-startup brand momentum; PageWheel competes on category fit + price.

shopify.name: Shopify
shopify.positioning: Ecommerce platform for selling physical/digital products. Bids broadly on website-builder and online-business keywords.
shopify.audience: Ecommerce sellers, DTC brands, physical/digital product businesses
shopify.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: Different category (ecommerce vs creator funnels). Both bid on "online business" / "sell digital products" terms but solve different problems. PageWheel is creator-first; Shopify is store-first.

squarespace.name: Squarespace
squarespace.tagline: Website builder for creatives, brands, and small businesses
squarespace.positioning: Design-forward website builder. Strong brand recognition. Bids on "website builder" and adjacent terms heavily.
squarespace.audience: Creatives, photographers, designers, small businesses, restaurants
squarespace.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: General website builder, no funnel/checkout/community focus. Different buyer; PageWheel competes on funnel-specific use cases vs Squarespace's general-site lane.

wix.name: Wix
wix.tagline: Create a website you're proud of
wix.positioning: Largest brand-name website builder. Heavy paid search spend. Wix bids on "page builder" and adjacent broad terms massively.
wix.differentiator_vs_pagewheel: Largest brand in this set — bids broadly on every website-builder keyword. PageWheel's wedge: AI-first templates targeted at creators (not generic SMBs) at half the price.

core_category.label: Core category — landing page + funnel builders
core_category.description: High-intent searches for the product category itself. PageWheel directly competes for these.

keywords.kw: drag and drop landing page builder

ai_assisted.label: AI page-builder searches — PageWheel's positioning sweet spot
ai_assisted.description: Searches with AI/automation intent. This is where PageWheel differentiates.

audience_specific.label: Audience-specific intent — coaches, course creators, authors

use_case.label: Use-case searches — what people are trying to build
use_case.description: Bottom-of-funnel searches with strong purchase intent.

keywords.kw: how to build a landing page
how to build a sales funnel
build a website for my business

brand_competitor.label: Branded competitor searches — opportunity for conquesting
brand_competitor.description: PageWheel can bid on these (review intent, comparison intent) to capture share-of-voice from competitor brand searches.

headline_research_summary.data_source_note: Volumes are researched estimates based on category knowledge and public Google Keyword Planner data points. For production planning, run these through the Hyder Media Google Keyword Planner endpoint at /api/google-ads/keywords for live volumes against the actual Digistore24 account.

clickfunnels.hero_headline: You're one funnel away from changing the world
clickfunnels.trust_signals: Customer logos, case studies, founder authority (Russell Brunson)
clickfunnels.design_style: Bold, brand-heavy, dark sections + bright accents. Heavy storytelling and authority signals throughout.
clickfunnels.form_fields: Email only on hero; pricing gated behind plan selection
clickfunnels.key_takeaway: Sells the dream first, pricing second. Heavy on storytelling, light on feature lists.

leadpages.hero_headline: Build pages that convert. No developer needed.
leadpages.trust_signals: Trustpilot rating, G2 badges, customer count
leadpages.design_style: Clean, modern, lots of product screenshots. Bright blue accent on white.
leadpages.pricing_visibility: Visible on dedicated pricing page; CTAs guide to it
leadpages.key_takeaway: Feature-first, product-screenshot-heavy. Clear positioning on AI + conversion.

kartra.hero_headline: Earn more with the most powerful all-in-one platform
kartra.trust_signals: Founder credibility (Andy Jenkins legacy), platform tour
kartra.design_style: Deep blue + orange accent. Heavy on platform tour graphics.
kartra.form_fields: Email + name on signup; checkout flow gated
kartra.page_length: Long — covers each feature in depth
kartra.pricing_visibility: Linked from nav; not on homepage hero
kartra.key_takeaway: Sells the breadth of integration (replaces 8-12 tools). Less storytelling than CF, more feature emphasis.

kajabi.hero_headline: Turn What You Know Into What You're Known For
kajabi.social_proof: 100K+ businesses, $11B+ earned by users, customer logos
kajabi.trust_signals: Earnings milestones, named creator success stories, 8-figure case studies
kajabi.design_style: Premium black + white + accent. Editorial photography. Quiet luxury vibe.
kajabi.page_length: Long — sells the dream of knowledge-business
kajabi.pricing_visibility: Hidden behind 'See plans' CTA; 50% promo emphasized
kajabi.key_takeaway: Aspirational and prestige-positioned. Sells the lifestyle/status of being a knowledge entrepreneur. Premium pricing reflects.

systemeio.hero_headline: The only tool you need to launch your online business
systemeio.design_style: Bright yellow + clean white. Cheerful, accessible, low-friction.
systemeio.key_takeaway: FREE-TIER-LED ACQUISITION. Direct counter to PageWheel's $27 starter — Systeme uses 'free forever' as the wedge.

gohighlevel.design_style: Dark theme with brand-accent. Heavy feature/screenshot grid.
gohighlevel.form_fields: Multi-step signup with agency name + use case
gohighlevel.key_takeaway: Agency platform, different buyer. Included for comparison/conquest opportunity only — PageWheel doesn't directly compete here.

base44.hero_headline: AI no-code platform — build apps with natural language
base44.social_proof: 44% off yearly promo, growing user base
base44.trust_signals: Israeli startup credibility, app gallery, customer logos
base44.design_style: Modern tech aesthetic, cyan accent on dark theme, motion graphics demonstrate AI generation in real-time
base44.pricing_visibility: Pricing in nav, free tier emphasized
base44.key_takeaway: PageWheel's AI-first positioning has direct competition here — but base44 is app-focused (internal tools, workflows) while PageWheel is page/funnel-focused. PageWheel can own the creator niche; base44 owns the operator niche.

elementor.hero_headline: The industry-leading visual website builder for WordPress
elementor.social_proof: 4M+ active WordPress installs, customer logos, community size
elementor.trust_signals: Massive install base, agency case studies, developer community
elementor.pricing_visibility: Free + paid tiers clear in nav
elementor.key_takeaway: Different buyer (WordPress committed). PageWheel's standalone all-in-one approach removes the WordPress prerequisite — easier first-time setup but no fallback into existing WP sites.

lovable.social_proof: Enterprise customer logos, template gallery, social mentions
lovable.trust_signals: AI-startup brand cachet, developer community, app showcase
lovable.design_style: Purple gradient, AI-startup minimalism, conversational UI demo
lovable.key_takeaway: Hot brand momentum in AI space. PageWheel wins where the buyer wants pages/funnels specifically (vs Lovable's broad app-builder positioning). Lovable is the brand-recognition leader in AI builders right now.

shopify.social_proof: Millions of merchants, customer earnings data, enterprise logos
shopify.trust_signals: Massive scale + ubiquitous brand recognition, GMV totals
shopify.design_style: Green accent on white, product/store screenshots, retail aesthetic
shopify.key_takeaway: Different category (ecommerce). Includes for keyword overlap on "online business" / "sell digital products" terms. PageWheel doesn't directly compete on inventory/shipping; PageWheel wins the digital-only creator.

squarespace.hero_headline: Website builder for creatives, brands, and small businesses
squarespace.social_proof: Customer logos (Keanu Reeves, John Malkovich brands), award wins
squarespace.trust_signals: Celebrity brand collaborations, design awards, longevity
squarespace.design_style: Editorial photography, black accent, beautiful template showcase
squarespace.key_takeaway: Design-first positioning aimed at creatives + brands. PageWheel's funnel/checkout/lead-magnet specialization is invisible here. They cross-bid heavily but solve different problems.

wix.hero_headline: Create a website you're proud of
wix.social_proof: Millions of users, customer site showcase, ad spend visible everywhere
wix.trust_signals: Massive brand recognition, AI/ADI feature emphasis, partner ecosystem
wix.design_style: Yellow accent, drag-and-drop demos prominent, big bold blocks
wix.key_takeaway: Largest brand spender in this set. Bids on every page-builder keyword. PageWheel's wedge: niche-specific to creators (not generic SMBs), AI-first templates with done-for-you setup, half the price.

pagewheel_pricing.ltv_basis: Starter $27 × 8mo = $216; Business $47 × 12mo = $564. Blended assumes 60/40 starter/business mix = $216×0.6 + $564×0.4 = $355. Round to $384 to account for upsells/annual conversions.

category_conversion_rates.basis: Category benchmark — SaaS w/ free trial typically converts 2.5-3% click→trial and 25-35% trial→paid. 0.75% click→paid is realistic for cold paid search.

spend_ramp_assumption.rationale: Lower-ticket SaaS — slower ramp than Digistore24 (which has high-LTV vendor signups). Steady scaling to find category CPCs and validate LTV assumptions.
